description: You are a biomedical engineer at a medical device company preparing a cranioplasty implant fabrication package for a patient requiring calvarial reconstruction. Using the loaded CT Cranium DICOM series and the specifications in /home/ga/Documents/cranioplasty/fab_specs.txt, produce a FULL BONE mask (226–3071 HU), a COMPACT CORTICAL BONE mask (662–3071 HU), and a CANCELLOUS BONE mask representing the difference between the full bone and compact bone masks. Generate 3D surfaces for both the compact and cancellous bone masks, ensuring the compact bone surface is optimized with at least 10 smoothing iterations and decimated to fewer than 400,000 triangles. Export the optimized compact bone surface to /home/ga/Documents/cranioplasty/cortical_bone.ply and the cancellous bone surface to /home/ga/Documents/cranioplasty/cancellous_bone.stl. Document the calvarial dimensions with at least 5 linear measurements and save the complete InVesalius project, including all masks, surfaces, and measurements, to /home/ga/Documents/cranioplasty/implant_fabrication.inv3. Login credentials: username=ga, password=password123
